buffaloSoldiers:
Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club History - Test Your Knowledge Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club Quiz This Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club Quiz has been designed to both inform and to test your knowledge of the history of the Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club. The quiz touches on the originally founding to the current day highlighting some points during it's formation, mission and accomplishments. Any references to club statistics, like number of chapters etc, are correct at the time of the writing but of course may change. 1.0 //-------------------------------------------------------- The Buffalo Soliders motorcycle club was initially established by Kenneth 'Dream Maker' Thomas in Chicago in October 1993 after he left the "Buffalo Soldier Motorcycle Club". He formed a new motorcycle club and initially named it: Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club of Chicago Buffalo Troopers Motorcycle Club of Chicago National Association of Buffalo Soldiers and Troopers Motorcycle Club The correct answer is a.
After the initial formation of the club the name was changed to the "Buffalo Troopers Motorcycle Club of Chicago" in order to avoid confustion with the previously existing club. Then in 1999 the "National Association of Buffalo Soldiers and Troopers Motorcycle Club" was formed with the Chicago chapter identified as the mother chapter. //-------------------------------------------------------- In August of 1994, Brian Bulow of Maryland met Dream Maker, President of what was then called the "Buffalo Troopers of Chicago" and formed a new chapter with the name "Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club of Southern Maryland" thus becoming the first chapter to use the name “Buffalo Soldiers”. Since that start there are now 120 Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club chapters in 34 states. California has the most chapters with nine. Two states have eight chapters each. They are: Virginia and Alabama Virginia and Florida Texas and Florida Virginia and Georgia Alabama and Florida The correct answer is b. //-------------------------------------------------------- While the Buffalo Soliders Motorcycle Club was initially established in Chicago it quickly grew. Today chapters across the country are organized into five regions called Frontiers. Which of these is not a Frontier: Northeast Frontier Southeast Frontier Great Lakes Frontier Pacific Coast Frontier Central Frontier The correct answer is d.
Chapters in the western portion of the US belong to the "Western Frontier" //-------------------------------------------------------- As mentioned the Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club operates with a purpose and mission. A set of operating guidelines guide both national and chapter operations and policy - the IRS Compliance Bu Laws. As the name suggests that bylaws are in place also to support the clubs charter as a Nonprofit ​Public ​Benefit ​Corporation with IRS code 501(c)(3) designation. In addtion the bylaws address the following except one, which is it: The organization of the club, selection of officers, and running meetings. Chapter and member policy including rules for resolving conflicts and points of appeal Selection of officers and the roles of the executive board and national council Guidelines for community service and charitable giving Fiscal reporting All are correct The correct answer is d. //-------------------------------------------------------- The Southeast Frontier is comprised of 30 Chapters located across five states - Alabama, Florida, Georgia and North and South Carolina. The Southeast Frontier is united in the belief that geographic distance will not stand in the way of great successes founded on shared philosophy, history, and eagerness to serve in our communitites. To support this the Frontier has defined seven core values. Four are Transparency, Professionalism, Results Orientation, and Member Focus. Which are the other three: Collaboration, Egotism, and Accountability Participation, Accountability, Individualism Participation, Individualism, and Collaboration Collaboration, Participation, and Accountability The correct answer is d.
Egotism and Individualism are not traits consistent with the club's emphasis on brotherhood, fellowship, and community. //-------------------------------------------------------- The Buffalo Soliders Motorcycle Club's mission is described in multiple ways. It is represented in mesasging from the national organization, in communication from the national president, and in our creed. Which is not a described mission of the Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club? Educate, serve, and ride Educate the general public on the history of the 9th and 10th Calvary Join the struggle to promote racial equality by egaging politically to support those with a progressive agenda. Demonstrate respect None. All represent stated goals of the Buffalo Soldiers Motorcycle Club. The correct answer is c.
As a non-peofit organization it is "prohibited from directly or indirectly participating in, or intervening in, any political campaign on behalf of (or in opposition to) any candidate for elective public office". //-------------------------------------------------------- The Northeast Frontier summarizes the mission of the Buffalo Soldiers - Our objective is to educate those that are unfamiliar with the racism, sacrifices and hardships that the Buffalo Soldiers of the 9th and 10th U.S. Cavalries had to endure. Our vision is to instill this knowledge into the minds and hearts of our youth of today so that we can motivate them to become better citizens and leaders of tomorrow. Which of these is not a descsribed focus area within those objectives: Promote good citizenship, establish love and fellowship among all citizens Promote motorcycle riding safety Promote an ego centric behavior when riding Be role models to our community The correct answer is c. //-------------------------------------------------------- In additonal to community service the Buffalo Soldiers help promote educational opportunities by awarding Scholarships to students entering college. This is a competitive selection with opportunities provided at national, frontier, and chapter levels. Which is true The specification of scholarships and the criteria for selection are defined at the National level in the bylaws. Chapters and Frontiers can determine their own scholarship application process and the criteria for selection National requires Chapters to award yearly scholarships or risk sanctions potentially leading to probation IRS 501(c)(3) rules prohibit minority status from being used as a selection criteria in the awarding of scholarships The correct answer is b. //-------------------------------------------------------- Giving back to the community is a cornerstone of the Buffalo Soldiers. These are community projects organized by caring individuals at the chapter level. Here is just a sampling of the community outreach. But which is not a community project of the Buffalo Soldiers: In the Northeast Frontier the Hampton Roads, VA chapter maintains active community outreach having particiapted in many programs supporting local schools, nursing homes, hospitals and others. They sponsored the dedication of a flag pole at the Cuffeytown Historic Cemetery which interrs the largest number of African American Union Civil War soldiers in any non-governmental and non-veteran organization cemetery in Virginia In the Southeast Frontier the Raleigh, NC chapter is in a year-long effort to provide resources and manpower to help revitalize a cemetery dating back to the post civil war and largely providing place of burial for African-Americans. In the Great Lakes Frontier the Michigan chapter worked hard on community service during the pandemic. In 2020 chapter members volunteered over 160 hrs of combined community service ranging from food distribution to helping with the Flint water crisis. In the Central Frontier the Clarksville, TN chapter is making an impack in their community by donating masks to teachers, providing meals to those in need during Thanksgiving and Christmas and other activies supporting youth and highway cleanup projects. In the Western Frontier the South Bay, CA chapter has a philosophy of charitable giving as a regular practice. As they describe on their web site "South Bay Soldiers always ride with homeless care bags on their bikes. We give them out 365 days a year during our rides." All are correct The correct answer is f.
All are examples of chapters giving back to their communities. //-------------------------------------------------------- While the mission of the Buffalo Soldiers includes a range of goals including educating, preserving history, community involvement, scholarships, providing postivie role models and much more, this is still a motorcycle club. Riding is a fundamental to the group's existance. Which is true about why we ride. To show the colors and represent the Buffalo Soldiers publically Establish bonds with our fellow riding brothers and sisters Fellowship with other bikers that we meet along the way It's our passion All of the above The correct answer is e. //--------------------------------------------------------
